title: Red Sox took advantage of MLB’s new automated strike challenge rules on Opening Day
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Red Sox utilized MLB's new Automated Ball-Strike Challenge System in a 3-0 Opening Day win over the Reds.
Roman Anthony's successful challenge overturned a strike call, leading to a two-out RBI single that extended the lead.
The ABS Challenge System allows players to contest umpire calls in real time, keeping successful challenges for later use.

title: In first Opening Day with Red Sox, Marcelo Mayer made quite the impression off the bench
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Marcelo Mayer made his Opening Day debut for the Red Sox as a pinch-hitter in Cincinnati, going 2-for-2.
Mayer helped Boston defeat the Reds 3-0 with a leadoff double and scored two runs during his time on the field.
Mayer’s roster spot was uncertain due to prior offensive challenges, but he impressed with his performance off the bench.

title: Lucky pup thanks NH firefighters for quick thinking that saved his life
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Ziggy the dog suffered a severe leg injury after cutting himself on a hidden solar light in New Hampshire.
On the way to a veterinary hospital, Ziggy's bleeding worsened, prompting a stop at Campton-Thornton Fire Department.
Firefighters applied a tourniquet, saving Ziggy's life; he later underwent surgery to repair the injury.

title: What’s causing my car to burn so much oil?
author: John Paul
bullets: A 2019 Subaru Legacy with 89,000 miles is consuming excessive oil without leaking.
Subaru recommended a costly $4,000 short block replacement to address the oil consumption.
The Car Doctor suggests checking oil control piston rings and the PCV system before replacing the engine.

title: This Nantucket diner just sold its fifth million dollar prize in the last 2 years
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Old South Diner in Nantucket has sold its fifth lottery ticket worth over $1 million since March 2024.
The diner received a $20,000 bonus for selling the latest $2 million Powerball ticket.
March 2026 saw two $2 million lottery wins at Old South Diner, with prizes cashed in Dorchester.

title: School bus driver in crash that killed child was allegedly involved in hit-and-run months later
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Jean Charles, 39, faces manslaughter and vehicular homicide charges in a crash killing 5-year-old Lens Arthur Joseph in Hyde Park.
Prosecutors accuse Charles of reckless driving and failing safety inspections, with defects noted that should have grounded the bus.
Months after the fatal crash, Charles was allegedly involved in a Brockton hit-and-run incident, facing separate charges.

title: 5 takeaways as Garrett Crochet, Marcelo Mayer steer Red Sox to 3-0 Opening Day win over Reds
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Garrett Crochet pitched six innings, allowing three hits and striking out eight, as the Red Sox defeated the Reds 3-0.
Marcelo Mayer contributed with a double and a single, helping the Red Sox pull ahead in the seventh inning.
Roman Anthony went 3-for-4, demonstrating strong plate discipline and aiding the Red Sox offensive efforts.

title: Body of Mass. man missing since escaping from a hospital in 2025 found in lake
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Leonard Mercury's body was found in Crystal Lake, Gardner, five months after he vanished from Heywood Hospital.
Mercury, 57, disappeared on October 31, 2025, after escaping the hospital in mental and medical distress.
Gardner police and the State Police Dive Team located and retrieved Mercury's body using drones on Wednesday.

title: A doctor once told her not to run. Now she’s taking on the Boston Marathon.
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Sydney Southern is running the 2026 Boston Marathon a decade after surgery for a giant cell tumor in her femur.
Southern, 33, from Peabody, was initially advised by her doctor against running due to the tumor.
She aims to raise funds for Mass General Hospital, which supported her recovery and restored her mobility.

title: Cambridge pediatric doctor running fifth Boston Marathon to support Mass General patients
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Jennifer Leah Goetz, a Cambridge pediatrician, is running her fifth Boston Marathon to support Massachusetts General Hospital patients.
Goetz trained in pediatrics and child psychiatry at MGH and was inspired by her work with young cancer patients.
She credits running with helping her overcome depression and is dedicated to raising funds for MGH through the marathon.
